---
name: objectives-backward-design
description: "Writing measurable learning objectives and applying backward design to align assessment and instruction."
---

# Objectives & Backward Design

Focus: define clear, measurable outcomes first and design assessments that provide evidence of those outcomes.

Core steps:
- Identify desired performance (what learners should be able to DO).
- Define acceptable evidence (assessment criteria, rubrics).
- Design learning experiences to scaffold toward the evidence.

Quick objective template (ABCD):
- Audience: who
- Behavior: observable action
- Condition: tools/constraints
- Degree: performance standard

Example: "Given an incomplete microservice and tests (Condition), a developer (Audience) will implement the missing endpoint (Behavior) so that all tests pass within 30 minutes (Degree)."

## Templates

Objective table (CSV):

Audience,Behavior,Condition,Degree
Developer,Implement endpoint,Given incomplete microservice and tests,All tests pass within 30 minutes

Quick alignment checklist:
- Objective present: Yes/No
- Evidence defined: Yes/No
- Assessment mapped: Yes/No
